whatThisIs: Show the pediatric cardiac arrest drug card from the AHA PALS algorithm with all doses weight-banded: epinephrine 0.01 mg/kg (0.1 mL/kg of 1:10,000) IV/IO every 3-5 minutes (max 1 mg per dose); amiodarone 5 mg/kg IV/IO bolus for refractory VF / pulseless VT, may repeat up to two times (max 15 mg/kg total or 300 mg per dose); lidocaine 1 mg/kg IV/IO loading; magnesium 25-50 mg/kg IV/IO (max 2 g) for torsades; calcium chloride 20 mg/kg and sodium bicarbonate 1 mEq/kg for specific reversible causes. Sophie Well prints the per-kg dose, the volume to draw at standard concentrations, and the maximum-per-dose cap side by side.
whenToUse: Use this in a pediatric resuscitation when the team needs immediate weight-banded doses without sliding rulers, or when a learner is rehearsing PALS pushes. The card is calculation reference - the algorithm (CPR ratio 15:2 with advanced airway, rhythm checks every 2 minutes, search for the H's and T's) still drives the team work, and the doses cap at adult amounts for older / larger pediatric patients.
